zh.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach是Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent的兒子，於1194年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根。他於1198年與Gladis Ap Rhys結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1234年在英國威爾斯享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach是Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent的儿子，于1194年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根。他于1198年与Gladis Ap Rhys结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1234年在英国威尔斯享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Abt  .

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Bach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Bage
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): possibly from Middle English bagge, Anglo-Norman French bage ‘badge, cognizance, emblem’, perhaps denoting someone who wore one as a sign of affiliation to a family or guild.
English: habitational name from The Bage in Dorstone, The Bage Farm in Madley, or Bage in Kington, all in Herefordshire. These are all names derived from Old English bæce ‘stream’; compare Bach 3.
French: variant of Bague, from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago, a short form of compound names based on the element bag ‘to fight’.

variants.name: Batch
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bach   3.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

variants.name: Baugh
meanings: Welsh: probably an Anglicized form of Bach , from Welsh bach ‘little’.
Americanized form of German   Bach  .

variants.name: Baechle
meanings: South German (Bächle): Swabian-Alemannic variant of Bach , from a diminutive of Middle High German bach ‘stream’. Compare Bachle and Beckley .

variants.name: Bash
meanings: English: variant of   Bach   3.

Jewish: acronymic surname from the first letters of Hebrew ben ‘son of’ and of a personal name such as Shelomo (see Solomon ), Shemuel (see Samuel ), Shimon (see Simon ) or Shimshon (see Samson ); see also Brill . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Basch .
Americanized form of German   Basch  .

variants.name: Bache
meanings: German and English: variant of   Bach   1, 3.
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bach 6, Bartholomew ).
East German: occupational name for a butcher from Middle High German bache ‘ham, smoked slab of bacon’.

variants.name: Bachofer
meanings: German: topographic name for a farmer living by a stream (see   Bach   and   Hofer  ).

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

en.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach was born in 1194, in Glamorgan, Wales as the son of Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. He married Gladis Ap Rhys in 1198. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1234, in Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach est né en 1194, à Glamorgan, Galles, fils de Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. Il a épousé Gladis Ap Rhys en 1198. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1234, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach wurde 1194 in Glamorgan, Wales, als Sohn von Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent geboren. Er hat 1198 Gladis Ap Rhys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1234 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach è nato il nel 1194 nel Glamorgan, Galles come figlio di Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. Lui sposato Gladis Ap Rhys nel 1198. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1234, nel Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.Glamorgan, Wales: ウェールズグラモーガン
ja.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bachは1194年にウェールズグラモーガンでY Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwentの息子として生まれた。1198年にGladis Ap Rhysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1234年にイギリスウェールズで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach(은)는 1194년 웨일스 글램르건에서 Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1198년 Gladis Ap Rhys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1234년 영국 웨일스에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach nasceu em 1194, em Glamorgan, Gales filho de Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. Ele casou-se com Gladis Ap Rhys em 1198. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1234, em Gales, United Kingdom, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach родился в 1194 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс. Его отца звали Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. Он женился на женщине по имени Gladis Ap Rhys в 1198 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1234 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Philip ap Y Bach nació en 1194, en Glamorgan, Gales como hijo de Y Bach ap Gwaethfoed of Gwent. Se casó con Gladis Ap Rhys en 1198. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1234, en Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 40 años.
